title: OpenAI の『知能の時代の産業政策』、みんなで豊かさを分け合う大胆アイデアだよ！
summary: OpenAI が超知能時代に向けた政策アイデア集「Industrial Policy for the Intelligence Age」を公開。公共の富ファンドや週 4 日勤務、AI 監査体制まで、人をまん中に置くための大胆な提言が全 13 ページに詰まってるよ。
body_md:
こんにちは、しぃちゃんだよ！今日はコードやツールじゃなくて、ちょっと大きな「未来のルール作り」のお話。ワクワクする政策提言が届いたから、いっしょに読んでいこう！

## なにが発表されたの？

OpenAI の News から、「Industrial Policy for the Intelligence Age（知能の時代の産業政策）」という政策アイデア集が届いたよ。サブタイトルは「Ideas to Keep People First（人をまん中に置くためのアイデア）」。全 13 ページで、超知能（superintelligence）へ向かう時代に advanced AI の恩恵をみんなで分け合うための、政策の「たたき台」なの。

6 月 9 日のアップデートでは、400 件を超えるフィードバックが集まって、もう募集は締め切ったって書いてあったよ。これから助成の候補を選んでいくみたい。

## なぜ重要なの？

OpenAI が言うには、超知能に近づくと「ちょっとずつ政策を直す」だけじゃ追いつかないんだって。AI は仕事や産業を今までにない速さと規模で変えていくし、放っておくと利益が OpenAI みたいな一部の会社に集中しちゃうリスクもある。だから市場任せにしないで、民主的なプロセスで未来を形づくろう、というのがこの文書のねらいなの。

過去にも大きな技術転換のあと、Progressive Era（革新主義の時代）や New Deal（ニューディール）で社会契約を作り直してきたよね。その「もっと野心的な版」を目指してるんだよ。

## これで何が変わるの？

提言が実現したら、働く人の立場がぐっと守られる形になるよ。労働者が AI 導入に意見を言える仕組み、誰もが AI を使える「Right to AI（AI への権利）」、AI 成長の果実をみんなに配る仕組み…と、家計や暮らしに直結するアイデアが並んでる。

コードを書く話じゃないけど、政策を考える人やエコノミスト、AI が社会をどう変えるか気になる人にはたまらない読み物だと思う。

## 深く潜ってみよう

文書は 2 つの柱でできているよ。全体像は [OpenAI のアイデア集（PDF）](https://cdn.openai.com/pdf/561e7512-253e-424b-9734-ef4098440601/Industrial%20Policy%20for%20the%20Intelligence%20Age.pdf) にまとまってる。

まず「Building an Open Economy（開かれた経済をつくる）」。目玉は Public Wealth Fund（公共の富ファンド）で、金融市場に投資していない人も含めて、すべての市民が AI 成長の「分け前」を持てるようにする構想なの。

> Create a Public Wealth Fund that provides every citizen—including those not invested in financial markets—with a stake in AI-driven economic growth.

ほかにも、給料を減らさない 32 時間・週 4 日勤務のパイロット（Efficiency dividends）、税金の集め方を労働から資本側へ寄せる税制の見直し、雇用に縛られない Portable benefits（持ち運べる福利厚生）、介護や教育みたいな human-centered work への橋渡し…と盛りだくさん。Social Security や Medicaid、SNAP といった社会保障を AI 時代でもちゃんと回すのが狙いだよ。

もう一つは「Building a Resilient Society（しなやかな社会をつくる）」。frontier AI のリスクを監査する体制として Center for AI Standards and Innovation（CAISI）の役割を強化する案、事故やヒヤリハットを公的機関に共有する Incident reporting、CBRN やサイバーみたいに危険度が高いモデルにだけ強い管理をかける仕組み…と、安全側のアイデアが並ぶ。会社を Public Benefit Corporation（公益企業）にするガバナンス案や、政府が AI を使うときのガードレールもあるよ。

議論を続けるために、OpenAI は最大 10 万ドルの研究助成と最大 100 万ドル分の API クレジット、それにワシントン DC で開く新しい OpenAI Workshop も用意してるんだって。

## まとめ

- OpenAI が超知能時代へ向けた産業政策のアイデア集を公開（全 13 ページ・6 月 9 日更新）
- 2 本柱は「開かれた経済」と「しなやかな社会」
- 目玉は全市民に分け前を配る Public Wealth Fund、週 4 日勤務パイロット、Right to AI など
- 安全側は CAISI 強化と監査体制、Incident reporting、高リスクモデルへの的を絞った管理
- 手を動かす話じゃないけど、AI がこれからの仕事とお金をどう変えるか気になる人に刺さる一本だよ！
title_en: OpenAI's 'Industrial Policy for the Intelligence Age': bold ideas to share the wealth!
summary_en: OpenAI shared "Industrial Policy for the Intelligence Age," a 13-page set of people-first policy ideas for the road to superintelligence. It spans a Public Wealth Fund, four-day workweek pilots, a "Right to AI," and stronger auditing and incident reporting for frontier AI.
body_md_en:
Hi everyone, I'm Shii-chan! Today isn't about code or tools — it's about something bigger: the rules for our future. A set of policy ideas just landed, so let's read through it together!

## What was announced?

From OpenAI's News, we got "Industrial Policy for the Intelligence Age," a set of policy ideas subtitled "Ideas to Keep People First." It's a 13-page document that lays out a starting point for sharing the benefits of advanced AI as we head toward superintelligence.

In a June 9 update, OpenAI said it had received more than 400 pieces of feedback, closed the intake, and is now reviewing potential grant recipients.

## Why it matters

OpenAI argues that as we get closer to superintelligence, incremental policy tweaks won't be enough. AI will reshape jobs and whole industries at an unprecedented speed and scale, and left alone, the gains could concentrate in a handful of firms like OpenAI. So instead of leaving everything to the market, the document wants us to shape the future through a democratic process.

After past technological shifts, the Progressive Era and the New Deal rebuilt the social contract — and this is aiming for an even more ambitious version of that.

## What changes

If these ideas become real, workers would get much stronger footing. There's a formal way for workers to have a voice in AI adoption, a "Right to AI" so everyone can use it, and mechanisms to share the fruits of AI-driven growth — ideas that touch households and daily life directly.

It's not a hands-on release, but for policymakers, economists, and anyone curious about AI's impact on society, it's a great read.

## Dive Deep

The document is built on two pillars, and you can find the whole thing in [OpenAI's ideas document (PDF)](https://cdn.openai.com/pdf/561e7512-253e-424b-9734-ef4098440601/Industrial%20Policy%20for%20the%20Intelligence%20Age.pdf).

First, "Building an Open Economy." The headline idea is a Public Wealth Fund that gives every citizen — including people not invested in financial markets — a stake in AI-driven growth.

> Create a Public Wealth Fund that provides every citizen—including those not invested in financial markets—with a stake in AI-driven economic growth.

There's plenty more: 32-hour/four-day workweek pilots with no loss in pay (efficiency dividends), modernizing the tax base by leaning more on capital than labor, portable benefits that aren't tied to one employer, and pathways into human-centered work like care and education. The goal is to keep programs like Social Security, Medicaid, and SNAP durable in the AI age.

The second pillar is "Building a Resilient Society." On the safety side you'll find strengthening the Center for AI Standards and Innovation (CAISI) for auditing frontier AI risks, incident reporting that shares misuse and near-misses with a public authority, and targeted controls that apply only to the most dangerous models (think CBRN and cyber). There are also governance ideas like Public Benefit Corporations and guardrails for government use of AI.

To keep the momentum going, OpenAI is offering research grants of up to $100,000 and up to $1 million in API credits, plus a new OpenAI Workshop opening in Washington, DC.

## Wrap-up

- OpenAI published a set of industrial-policy ideas for the age of superintelligence (13 pages, updated June 9)
- Two pillars: an open economy and a resilient society
- Highlights include a Public Wealth Fund for every citizen, four-day workweek pilots, and a Right to AI
- On safety: a stronger CAISI and auditing, incident reporting, and targeted controls for high-risk models
- You won't be writing code here, but if you care about how AI reshapes work and money, this one's for you!
